sim_hash_policy_note: Re-baselined 2026-07-23 for FID-0046 opaque-pool pointer canonicalization. The widescreen opt-out remains a distinct, stable render-bookkeeping anchor; gameplay/input is unchanged.
note: Archetype traversal route on runway (Phase B, FID-0031/D4). Bond walks forward, strafes right, then turns (GE007_AUTO_FORWARD/RIGHT/CRIGHT baked into the captured OSContPad stream) with the level intro skipped (replay_env) so the movement window exercises the level geometry/collision sim. Deterministic under the determinism envelope + replay_env: record==replay1==replay2 (sim_state_hash byte-exact, verified across independent recordings). The ares-free determinism half of the runway gate:true route; the ares trace comparison is the manual oracle step (movement_oracle_capture.sh --route runway_traverse). Regenerate only via scratchpad/finalize_archetype_tapes.sh. Gated by tools/fidelity/gate_routes_smoke.sh + tools/fidelity/tape_regression.sh. Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (b94a6243900d552e→7ce3a55a6b47b00a): near the end of this route's window a guard (chr=24, item=8/KF7, AutomaticFiringRate=3) enters sustained full-auto line-of-sight on Bond (GE007_TRACE_GUARD_AUTOFIRE: 2 gate-fires at eff_rate=9). The recorded b94a6243900d552e was captured (commit 9e661b3) against a stale pre-FID-0066 ge007 binary (linked before the FID-0066 chrlv.c fix landed, despite the source tree already containing it) and is exactly the legacy (GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0) hash -- kept here as fire_rate_authentic_off_hash, verified byte-identical on both a git-archive build of b289b60 (the pre-merge-train tip) and the current tree's opt-out replay. bunker1_traverse/facility_traverse/surface1_traverse carry zero guard-autofire events in their windows and are unaffected (hashes unchanged). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0066-npc-fire-rate.md and the P1a/Phase-B repair evidence for the full attribution. Re-baselined 2026-07-11 (7ce3a55a6b47b00a->56c32149572a6055) for FID-0014: the faithful WAYMODE_MAGIC patrol semantics (default ON) freeze unseen patrolling/gopos guards and pad-warp them per retail chrTickBeams US 0x7F020EF0 / chrlvTravelTickMagic US 0x7F028600, so offscreen patroller positions (sim state) differ from the legacy creep-walk. GE007_NO_PATROL_MAGIC_FIX=1 reproduces the previous hash 7ce3a55a6b47b00a byte-identically (verified on this tape). See docs/fidelity/derivations/FID-0014-patrol-magic.md. fire_rate_authentic_off_hash likewise re-based b94a6243900d552e->ff4975b85d2d1ca1 (= fix-ON default + GE007_FIRE_RATE_AUTHENTIC=0; the FID-0066 opt-out contract is unchanged). Re-baselined 2026-07-12 (56c32149572a6055->75689965bb24dff9) for FID-0117: the root-motion seed flag in modelSetAnimFrame2WithChrStuff now reads rwdata->unk01 (offset 1, retail) instead of the port's erroneous rwdata->unk02 (offset 2). The flag is written to unk01 by modelSetAnimation2 and read at offset 1 by retail (ASM 7F0702EC: lb 1(v0), stored to sp+0x94==save_unk01); the port read the wrong adjacent byte, altering character root-motion accumulation on animation transitions. GE007_NO_ANIMFRAME2_ROOTFLAG_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash 56c32149572a6055 byte-identically (A/B verified across all 7 tapes). Faithful sim change, not a regression. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0117.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-19 (75689965bb24dff9->fc6ac908e81a03a7) for FID-0132: the collision-hull builder sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 now stores the retail (unclamped) hull vertex count on the collision_data-backed object/door path (rect==&data->unk04, spare words unk24..unk40 are the retail overflow slots); the FID-0096 clamp-to-4 remains only on the bare-rect4f tank path. The old unconditional clamp dropped real hull corners for thin/near-axis-aligned box footprints (degenerate collision polygons -> broken point-in-polygon -> object grounding errors, e.g. the Dam control-room monitors floor-snapping 93 units below their desks; 116 spurious-degenerate footprints across dam/facility/archives/streets -> 0). Corrected footprints change object collision/grounding sim state on every object-bearing level. GE007_NO_HULL_CORNER_FIX=1 reproduces the prior hash 75689965bb24dff9 byte-identically (verified on this tape on the fixed binary). Faithful sim change, not a regression. Corpus ctest hull_builder guards the geometry. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0132.json. Re-baselined 2026-07-22 (fc6ac908e81a03a7->c3ad0defd0ae0ec3) for FID-0143: the widescreen draw-only visibility widen (default ON) renders guards standing in the widened 16:9 side strips; their render writes the transient chr->field_20 render-DL pointer inside the pool-hashed chr records. GAMEPLAY IS PROVEN UNCHANGED: per-frame canonicalized dumps of prop_pool and g_BgRoomInfo are byte-identical widen-on vs widen-off across all 361 frames of this tape; the pool delta was attributed to the byte via an lldb write-watchpoint (chrTickBeams DL-chain store, chr+0x40). GE007_NO_WIDESCREEN_VIS_WIDEN=1 reproduces the prior hash fc6ac908e81a03a7 byte-identically (4/4 runs; pinned as the widescreen_widen_optout variant below). Render-bookkeeping move, not a regression. See docs/fidelity/ledger/FID-0143.json.

variants.name: widescreen_widen_optout
variants.note: FID-0143 opt-out reproduction contract: with the widescreen widen disabled this tape must reproduce the pre-widen hash byte-identically — the same-binary attribution anchor (FID-0132 discipline) proving the default-world delta is the widen's render-bookkeeping only.
